name: MSW resonance and adiabatic flavor conversion in the Sun
requirement: Any candidate framework must reproduce the measured solar electron-neutrino survival probability P_ee(E) across the full pp-chain energy range (pp at ~0.1 MeV through 8B at ~10 MeV), including the characteristic MSW transition: low-energy vacuum-dominated regime with P_ee ~ 1 - sin^2(2 theta_12)/2 ~ 0.55, high-energy matter-dominated (adiabatic) regime with P_ee ~ sin^2(theta_12) ~ 0.30, and the rising 'upturn' through 1-5 MeV. The Borexino spectroscopic measurements of pp, 7Be, pep, 8B, and CNO solar-neutrino fluxes plus the SNO neutral-current 8B flux establish the MSW-LMA solution at >5 sigma over a vacuum-only oscillation, fixing Delta m^2_21 = (7.42 +/- 0.21) x 10^-5 eV^2 and sin^2(theta_12) = 0.304 +/- 0.012 (NuFIT 6.0).

regime.description: Solar core (rho ~ 150 g/cm^3, electron density n_e ~ 6 x 10^25 cm^-3) to vacuum propagation over 1 AU. Adiabatic resonance at the layer where 2 sqrt(2) G_F n_e E = Delta m^2_21 cos(2 theta_12); for the high-energy 8B neutrinos (E ~ 10 MeV, taken as the characteristic energy) this layer lies in the radiative core. Full energy range probed: ~0.1 MeV (pp) to ~15 MeV (hep tail).

current_bound.value: Delta m^2_21 = (7.42 +0.21 -0.20) x 10^-5 eV^2; sin^2(theta_12) = 0.304 +/- 0.012; P_ee at 8B ~0.32 (Borexino + SNO + Super-K), at pp ~0.57 (Borexino), with the predicted MSW upturn structure observed
current_bound.units: eV^2 (mass splitting); dimensionless (mixing angle, survival probability)
current_bound.experiment: SNO (NC, CC, ES), Super-Kamiokande (8B, day-night), Borexino (pp, 7Be, pep, 8B, CNO), KamLAND (reactor anti-nu_e disappearance, fixes Delta m^2_21)
current_bound.reference: Ahmad et al. (SNO), 'Direct Evidence for Neutrino Flavor Transformation from Neutral-Current Interactions in the Sudbury Neutrino Observatory', PRL 89, 011301 (2002), arXiv:nucl-ex/0204008; Borexino Collaboration, 'Comprehensive measurement of pp-chain solar neutrinos', Nature 562, 505 (2018); 'Experimental evidence of neutrinos produced in the CNO fusion cycle in the Sun', Nature 587, 577 (2020); NuFIT 6.0 (Esteban et al., JHEP 12, 216 (2024), arXiv:2410.05380)

what_it_excludes: Three classes of alternatives: (1) pure vacuum oscillation with the same Delta m^2_21 and theta_12 - excluded because it would not produce the energy-dependent transition between low-E and high-E P_ee. (2) 'Sterile-only' or 'Majoron decay' explanations of the original solar neutrino deficit - all excluded since SNO's neutral-current measurement showed total flux equals SSM. (3) Frameworks that change the Sun's interior matter potential (modified weak interactions, non-standard neutrino-electron couplings (NSI), light dark-matter modification of solar density). NSI parameters are bounded at the few-percent level. Together with reactor neutrino measurements (KamLAND fixes Delta m^2_21 independently of solar matter effects), this benchmark is the cleanest test of MSW-style adiabatic flavor conversion - the textbook prediction of three-flavor neutrino oscillation in matter.

parameterization.name: Three-flavor neutrino propagation in matter, MSW Hamiltonian H = H_vac + sqrt(2) G_F n_e diag(1, 0, 0). Standard parameterization in (theta_12, theta_13, theta_23, delta_CP, Delta m^2_21, Delta m^2_31).
parameterization.reference: Wolfenstein, PRD 17, 2369 (1978); Mikheev and Smirnov, Sov. J. Nucl. Phys. 42, 913 (1985); Bahcall, Pinsonneault, Basu, ApJ 555, 990 (2001) (B16-AGSS09 / B16-GS98 Standard Solar Models).
parameterization.summary: Solar electron-neutrinos are produced in pp-chain (pp, pep, 7Be, 8B, hep) and CNO-cycle (13N, 15O, 17F) reactions with calculable fluxes. They propagate adiabatically through the solar density profile, undergo MSW resonance for E > ~2 MeV, and free-stream to Earth. P_ee(E) integrates over the production-region density distribution from the Standard Solar Model.

procedural.predicted_observable: Solar nu_e survival probability P_ee(E), measured by Borexino (low-E) and SNO/SK (high-E); CC/NC ratio at SNO; day-night asymmetry at SK and SNO (regeneration through Earth matter).
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: Delta m^2_21 = 7.42(21) x 10^-5 eV^2, sin^2(theta_12) = 0.304(12); NSI parameters epsilon_ee^uV, epsilon_ee^dV bounded at the 1-10% level; sterile-neutrino mixing into solar nu_e disfavored at delta theta < ~10^-3 for sterile mass splittings in the relevant range.
framework_inputs: If the framework predicts neutrino masses and mixings, derive theta_12 and Delta m^2_21 and confront with NuFIT.
If the framework predicts non-standard neutrino-matter interactions, include them in the matter Hamiltonian and recompute P_ee(E).

If the framework alters the solar interior (e.g. light dark matter changing energy transport, modified neutrino-electron weak coupling), recompute the Standard Solar Model fluxes and solar density profile.
Required: prediction of P_ee(E) over 0.1-15 MeV with the MSW upturn through 1-5 MeV reproduced.
procedural.notes: The MSW-LMA solution is now textbook physics, but it is also the most precision-tested example of in-matter adiabatic flavor conversion. Future JUNO will measure the solar neutrinos with sub-percent precision and probe the MSW upturn at higher significance; Hyper-K and DUNE will refine. The MSW mechanism also operates in supernova neutrinos and in the early universe; reproducing the solar bound is a necessary but not sufficient condition for handling those regimes.

primary_references.citation: Ahmad et al. (SNO Collaboration), 'Direct Evidence for Neutrino Flavor Transformation from Neutral-Current Interactions in the Sudbury Neutrino Observatory', PRL 89, 011301 (2002)
Borexino Collaboration, 'Comprehensive measurement of pp-chain solar neutrinos', Nature 562, 505 (2018)
Borexino Collaboration, 'Experimental evidence of neutrinos produced in the CNO fusion cycle in the Sun', Nature 587, 577 (2020)
Esteban, Gonzalez-Garcia, Maltoni, Martinez-Soler, Pinheiro, Schwetz (NuFIT 6.0), 'Updated global analysis of three-flavor neutrino oscillations', JHEP 12, 216 (2024)
Mikheev and Smirnov, 'Resonance enhancement of oscillations in matter and solar neutrino spectroscopy', Sov. J. Nucl. Phys. 42, 913 (1985)
